pattern: Counting how many elements sit at each power-of-two level and sweeping the target's bits from low to high while carrying leftover pairs upward turns 'can this subset of split pieces reach the target' into a single pass with on-demand splitting.
hints: Group elements by which power of two they equal; two elements at the same level can substitute for one element at the next level up when you add them into your chosen subset.

Sweep the target's bits from the lowest to the highest, keeping a running 'carry' of leftover same-level elements that pair up to help satisfy the next bit -- this models substitution without ever performing a split.

Only when a bit is required and neither the existing count nor the carried-forward leftovers can supply it do you actually need to spend an operation, cascading a single element down one level at a time from the nearest higher non-empty level.

edge_inputs.description: one split unlocks the target sum
two splits are needed across different levels
total sum falls short of the target
a single element exactly equal to the target
the target is exactly the total sum, needing everything
the target is already directly reachable, so zero splits should be reported
several small elements can substitute for a missing higher level without any split
the only large element must cascade down several levels to reach a low bit

stress.description: small random multisets of small powers of two, for a state-space cross-check
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 6))
    nums = [2 ** rng.randint(0, 5) for _ in range(n)]
    target = rng.randint(1, 60)
    return [nums, target]
stress.note: kept small enough for an exhaustive state-space oracle during authoring

stress.description: large random multisets at the full constraint scale
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 1000))
    nums = [2 ** rng.randint(0, 30) for _ in range(n)]
    target = rng.randint(1, 2**31 - 1)
    return [nums, target]
stress.note: exercises deep cascades across many levels

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def minOperations(self, nums, target):
        total = sum(nums)
        if total < target:
            return -1
        MAXLVL = 45
        have = [0] * (MAXLVL + 2)
        for v in nums:
            b = v.bit_length() - 1
            have[b] += 1
        ops = 0
        carry = 0
        for i in range(0, MAXLVL + 1):
            bit = (target >> i) & 1
            avail = have[i] + carry
            if avail < bit:
                j = i + 1
                while j <= MAXLVL + 1 and have[j] == 0:
                    j += 1
                while j > i:
                    have[j] -= 1
                    have[j - 1] += 2
                    ops += 1
                    j -= 1
                avail = have[i] + carry
            carry = (avail - bit) // 2
        return ops
